 {"id":"br-2bbt.4.3","title":"Replace PercentileRibbon with native chart composition","description":"Replace hand-rolled PercentileRibbon with frankentui chart widget composition:

1. PercentileRibbon (tui_widgets.rs):
   - Currently: manually renders p50/p95/p99 latency bands with custom coloring
   - Replace with: Three stacked ftui_extras::charts::Sparkline widgets OR
     single BarChart with grouped bars per time bucket
   - Each percentile tier has distinct color (p50=green, p95=yellow, p99=red)

2. Apply to ToolMetrics screen (tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs):
   - Dashboard view uses PercentileRibbon for latency visualization
   - Replace in-place, preserving data flow and update frequency

3. Consider alternative: HeatmapGrid with time on x-axis, percentile on y-axis
   - Each cell colored by latency value
   - More information-dense than ribbons

## Tests
- Snapshot: ribbon with sample latency data across 20 time buckets
- Unit: percentile data aggregation into chart data points
- Visual comparison: old ribbon vs new chart composition","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:51:33.993718903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T00:51:33.993718903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charts","frankentui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:51:33.993718903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.4.4","title":"Remove deprecated custom widget code from tui_widgets.rs","description":"After all widget replacements are verified, clean up tui_widgets.rs:

NOTE: This bead removes CODE from within tui_widgets.rs (editing the file).
It does NOT delete the tui_widgets.rs file itself. Per AGENTS.md Rule 1,
file deletion requires express permission. This is code editing, not file deletion.

1. Remove BrailleActivity implementation from tui_widgets.rs (replaced by Sparkline)
2. Remove ReservationGauge implementation from tui_widgets.rs (replaced by ProgressBar)
3. Remove PercentileRibbon implementation from tui_widgets.rs (replaced by chart composition)
4. Update MetricTile to use new Sparkline internally (keep MetricTile, change its internals)
5. Update HeatmapGrid to use ftui_extras heatmap_gradient if available
6. Remove any utility functions that were only used by the 3 deprecated widgets
7. Update imports in all screen files that referenced the old widgets

Goal: reduce tui_widgets.rs by 150+ lines of custom rendering code while
maintaining ALL visual functionality through the new native widgets.

Widgets that REMAIN (not removed):
- MetricTile (updated internals)
- Leaderboard
- AnomalyCard
- AgentHeatmap
- Any other widgets not listed for replacement

## Tests
- Cargo check passes (no unused imports/functions)
- Clippy clean (no dead code warnings)
- All existing TUI tests still pass
- All snapshot tests match expected output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:51:34.162096833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:50:54.465293547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:51:34.162096833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:09.656289293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:09.826963416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:09.997853882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.5","title":"[track] Modal Dialogs and Contextual Action Menus","description":"## Rationale

Two UX problems exist in the current TUI:

1. Destructive actions (force-release reservation, clear filters) happen with a single keypress and no confirmation. This is risky in a multi-agent environment where accidentally releasing a reservation can cause editing conflicts.

2. When selecting an item (message, reservation, agent), the available actions require memorizing per-screen keybindings. There is no discoverable way to see what you can do with a selected item.

frankentui provides modal dialog widgets with focus management, action buttons, and keyboard navigation. Combined with contextual action menus, this dramatically improves both safety and discoverability.

## Implementation Approach

1. Modal Confirmation Dialogs:
   - Before force-release: show reservation details + confirm/cancel
   - Before clear-and-reset: show warning + type confirmation phrase
   - Modal renders as centered overlay with border
   - Focus trapped inside modal until resolved
   - Esc always cancels

2. Contextual Action Menu:
   - Press Enter on selected item -> show action menu overlay
   - Messages screen: View body, Acknowledge, Reply (copy thread_id), Jump to thread, Jump to sender
   - Reservations screen: Renew, Release, Force-release, View holder
   - Agents screen: View profile, Send message, View inbox, View reservations
   - Threads screen: View messages, Summarize, Search in thread
   - Action menu rendered as floating list near selected item

3. Integration with Command Palette:
   - Context-sensitive actions also appear in command palette when item is selected
   - Palette shows item-specific actions first, then global actions

## User Perception

Feels intentional and safe. Expert users keep using direct keybindings (Enter is additive). New users discover all available actions organically. Destructive actions require conscious confirmation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Modal confirmation dialog appears before destructive actions
- Modal traps focus and handles Esc (cancel) / Enter (confirm)
- Action menu appears on Enter with correct per-screen actions
- Action menu closes after selection or on Esc
- All actions in menu actually work (navigate, execute, etc.)
- Direct keybindings still work as shortcuts (bypass menu)
- Works at all terminal sizes
- Unit tests for ModalManager, ActionMenuBuilder, focus trapping
- Snapshot tests for modal rendering and action menu rendering
- E2E test: select reservation, press Enter, choose force-release, confirm in modal","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:49:47.776581502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T00:49:47.776581502Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","modals","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:49:47.776581502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.5.1","title":"Create ModalManager for confirmation dialogs","description":"Create ModalManager struct for managing modal dialog lifecycle:

1. ModalManager fields: active_modal (Option<Modal>), result_callback
2. Modal struct: title, body_lines, actions (Vec<ModalAction>), selected_action_index, severity
3. ModalAction: label, action_type (Confirm/Cancel/Custom), keybinding

Behavior:
- show_confirmation(title, body, on_confirm) -> renders centered overlay
- Focus trapped inside modal (all key events go to modal, not screen)
- Enter executes selected action, Esc always cancels
- Tab/arrow keys navigate between actions
- Modal renders with border, severity-colored title bar, action buttons at bottom

Pre-built modals:
- force_release_confirmation(reservation_details) -> confirm/cancel
- clear_all_confirmation(warning_text) -> confirm/cancel  
- destructive_action_confirmation(action_name, details) -> confirm/cancel

## Tests
- Unit: focus trapping (key events not passed to parent)
- Unit: Esc always cancels regardless of selected action
- Unit: Enter executes selected action
- Snapshot: modal rendering centered in Large/Compact terminals
- Snapshot: modal with 2 actions, 3 actions, long body text","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.414848124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.414848124Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modals","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.414848124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2bbt.5.1","title":"Create ModalManager for confirmation dialogs","description":"Create ModalManager for managing modal dialog lifecycle:

1. ModalManager fields: active_modal (Option<Modal>), result_callback
2. Modal struct: title, body_lines, actions (Vec<ModalAction>), selected_action_index, severity
3. ModalAction: label, action_type (Confirm/Cancel/Custom), keybinding

Behavior:
- show_confirmation(title, body, on_confirm) -> renders centered overlay
- Focus trapped inside modal (all key events go to modal, not screen)
- Enter executes selected action, Esc always cancels
- Tab/arrow keys navigate between actions
- Modal renders with border, severity-colored title bar, action buttons at bottom

Pre-built modals:
- force_release_confirmation(reservation_details) -> confirm/cancel
- clear_all_confirmation(warning_text) -> confirm/cancel
- send_confirmation(message_summary) -> confirm/cancel (for compose panel)
- destructive_action_confirmation(action_name, details) -> confirm/cancel

## File Location Guidance
Add ModalManager to tui_app.rs as a new field of MailAppModel (similar to how
command_palette and notifications are already fields). The modal rendering goes
in the view function after screen rendering but before palette rendering.
Z-order: screens < toasts < modals < command palette.

Only create a separate tui_modal.rs if the modal code grows beyond ~300 lines.

## Tests
- Unit: focus trapping (key events not passed to parent)
- Unit: Esc always cancels regardless of selected action
- Unit: Enter executes selected action
- Snapshot: modal rendering centered in Large/Compact terminals
- Snapshot: modal with 2 actions, 3 actions, long body text","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.414848124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:02:54.374746091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modals","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.414848124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.5.2","title":"Create ActionMenu for contextual per-item actions","description":"Create ActionMenu overlay for contextual actions on selected items:

1. ActionMenu struct: entries (Vec<ActionEntry>), selected_index, anchor_position
2. ActionEntry: label, description, keybinding (Option<String>), action closure, is_destructive flag

Per-screen action sets:
- Messages: View body | Acknowledge | Mark read | Jump to thread | Jump to sender
- Reservations: Renew | Release | Force-release (-> modal) | View holder agent
- Agents: View profile | View inbox | View reservations | Send message
- Threads: View messages | Summarize | Search in thread
- Timeline: View details | Filter by type | Filter by source | Copy event text
- Contacts: View agent | Approve | Deny | Block

Trigger key: . (period) on selected item opens ActionMenu as floating overlay near item.
Rationale: Enter is reserved for primary action (expand/navigate/select). Period is the standard action-menu trigger in tools like GitHub CLI and vim. This avoids collision with deep-linking (Enter) and text input (Enter in compose/search).

Behavior:
- . on selected item opens ActionMenu anchored near the selected row
- Arrow keys navigate menu entries
- Enter executes selected action
- Esc closes menu without action
- Typing first letter of action label jumps to it (e.g., a -> Acknowledge)
- Menu shows keybinding shortcuts next to each entry
- Destructive actions marked with red text and trigger confirmation modal

## Tests
- Unit: per-screen action set correctness (each screen returns correct actions)
- Unit: first-letter jump navigation (typing a selects Acknowledge)
- Unit: is_destructive flag correctly set on force-release, block, clear actions
- Snapshot: action menu rendering anchored near row 5 in Large terminal
- Snapshot: action menu with 6 entries showing keybinding hints
- E2E: select message, press ., verify menu appears with correct actions, select Acknowledge, verify ack executed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.592124628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:18:38.957923314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modals","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.592124628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.5.3","title":"Wire modals into destructive actions across all screens","description":"Connect ModalManager to all destructive actions in the TUI:

1. Reservations screen:
   - Force-release (current: direct keypress) -> now shows confirmation modal
   - Modal shows: reservation path, holder agent, TTL remaining
   - On confirm: execute force-release, show success toast

2. Explorer/Messages:
   - Clear all filters (if any clear-all action exists) -> confirmation modal
   
3. SystemHealth:
   - Any reset/restart action -> confirmation modal

4. Integration with ActionMenu:
   - When action is destructive, ActionMenu entry triggers modal instead of direct execution
   - Action entries marked with is_destructive flag

5. Ensure all modals emit toast on completion (success or cancellation logged)

## Tests
- E2E: force-release via action menu -> modal appears -> confirm -> reservation released -> toast shown
- E2E: force-release via action menu -> modal appears -> Esc -> reservation unchanged
- Unit: is_destructive flag correctly set on force-release, clear, reset actions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.768148321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:10.701726967Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modals","safety","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:10.701687253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:00.768148321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:10.174443753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:10.353202137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.6","title":"[track] Animated State Transitions","description":"## Rationale (expanded with webapp inspiration)

The webapp uses CSS animations extensively: Urgent importance badges PULSE with animation (0.75s infinite), staggered card entrance animations on Projects page, hover effects with smooth transitions (300ms ease), activity feed items animate in with stagger delays, network graph nodes have physics-based movement.

Static displays in the TUI feel dead by comparison. frankentui provides: ftui_extras text_effects (color gradients, reveal modes, cursor styles), animation-aware rendering with tick-driven state, accessibility support via AM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION.

## Specific Animations to Implement

1. Urgent badge pulse (from webapp): Messages with importance=urgent get pulsing red badge. Cycle: bright red -> dim red -> bright red (1Hz). Appears on Dashboard, Messages list, Thread cards.

2. New message flash (from webapp activity feed): When a new message arrives, briefly highlight the row. Flash duration: 300ms (inverted colors, then fade to normal).

3. Reservation TTL color gradient (from webapp): Smooth transition green (>60%) -> yellow (30-60%) -> orange (10-30%) -> red (<10%). Updates every tick, not just at threshold crossings.

4. Agent status transition (from webapp hover effects): When agent becomes idle/inactive, fade color over 5 ticks. Active: bright, Idle: dimmed, Inactive: very dim.

5. Toast entrance/exit (from webapp): Entrance: slide in from right (4 ticks). Exit: fade out over 3 ticks.

6. Staggered list load (from webapp card entrance): New content appears with cascade effect (1 tick per row, max 10 ticks).

## Implementation
All animations are tick-driven (no separate threads). AnimState { phase: f32, active: bool } per widget. AM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION=true skips all (instant final state). O(1) per tick per widget.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 4 of 6 animations implemented
- All respect reduced-motion setting
- No performance impact
- Unit tests for animation state machine
- Snapshot tests with fixed tick count","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.338061376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:12.248274035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["animation","polish","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.6","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.338061376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.6","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:53.856523431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
